channel.title: math.AC updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.AC updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Varieties with Ulrich exterior powers of the tangent bundle
item.description: arXiv:2606.27851v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We study smooth polarized projective varieties $(X,H)$ whose exterior powers of the tangent bundle are Ulrich. We prove that if $\bigwedge^rT_X$ is $H$-Ulrich for some $0<r<\dim X$, then $X$ is Fano and the intersection number $(-K_X)\cdot H^{n-1}$ is determined explicitly. We then classify the Picard number one case: the only example is the Veronese surface $(\mathbb P^2,\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^2}(2))$.
item.dc:creator: Yuta Takahashi, Kiwamu Watanabe

item.title: Applications of reduced and coreduced modules II: Radicality of the functor $\text{Hom}_R(R/I, -)$
item.description: arXiv:2306.12871v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: This is the second in a series of papers highlighting the applications of reduced and coreduced modules. Let $R$ be a commutative unital ring and $I$ be an ideal of $R$. We give necessary and sufficient conditions in terms of $I$-reduced $R$-modules for the functor $\text{Hom}_R(R/I, -)$ on an Abelian full subcategory of the category of $R$-modules to be a radical. $I$-reduced and $I$-coreduced $R$-modules provide a natural setting for a generalisation of Jans' correspondence, and lead to the construction of a new radical class of rings.
item.dc:creator: David Ssevviiri

item.title: The Amalgamation Property for automorphisms of ordered abelian groups
item.description: arXiv:2209.03944v5 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We prove that the category of ordered abelian groups equipped with an automorphism has the Amalgamation Property, deduce that their inductive theory is NIP in the sense of positive logic, and initiate a development of the latter framework. As byproducts of the proof, we obtain a generalised version of the Hahn Embedding Theorem which allows to lift each automorphism of an ordered abelian group to one of an ordered real vector space, and we show that, on existentially closed structures, linear combinations of iterates of the automorphism have the Intermediate Value Property.
item.dc:creator: Jan Dobrowolski, Rosario Mennuni

item.title: Fine multidegrees, universal Grobner bases, and matrix Schubert varieties
item.description: arXiv:2410.02135v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We give a criterion for a collection of polynomials to be a universal Gr\"{o}bner basis for an ideal in terms of the multidegree of the closure of the corresponding affine variety in $(\mathbb{P}^1)^N$. This criterion can be used to give simple proofs of several existing results on universal Gr\"{o}bner bases. We introduce fine Schubert polynomials, which record the multidegrees of the closures of matrix Schubert varieties in $(\mathbb{P}^1)^{n^2}$. We compute the fine Schubert polynomials of permutations $w$ where the coefficients of the Schubert polynomials of $w$ and $w^{-1}$ are all either 0 or 1, and we use this to give a universal Gr\"{o}bner basis for the ideal of the matrix Schubert variety of such a permutation.
item.dc:creator: Daoji Huang, Matt Larson

item.title: The Isomorphism Classes of the Surfaces $x_1^{a_1} + x_2^{a_2} + x_3^{a_3} + 1 = 0$
item.description: arXiv:2605.07617v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Let $f = x_1^{a_1} + x_2^{a_2} + x_3^{a_3} + 1 \in \mathbb{C}[x_1,x_2,x_3]$ and let $g = y_1^{b_1} + y_2^{b_2} + y_3^{b_3} + 1 \in \mathbb{C}[y_1,y_2,y_3]$ where $a_1,a_2,a_3,b_1,b_2,b_3 \geq 2$. We prove that the surfaces $V(f) \subset \mathbb{A}^3$ and $V(g) \subset \mathbb{A}^3$ are isomorphic if and only if $(a_1,a_2,a_3) = (b_1,b_2,b_3)$ up to a permutation of the entries.
item.dc:creator: Michael Chitayat, Buddhadev Hajra
